# Remove the variable-overrides comment

**Date:** 2026-06-24
**Status:** Approved

## Problem

Every file rendered from a template carries two autogenerated comment lines:

```yaml
# This file is autogenerated from https://github.com/Invoca/web/blob/-/templates/.../foo-deploy.jsonnet
# Variable overrides used: {"deploySha":"c1b207207834863ba96ffd130f1b1d03c0406672"}
```

`deploySha` is different on every build, so the `# Variable overrides used:` line
changes on **every render of every file** — including files (e.g. a Service) that
never reference `deploySha`. When a render touches many files, this produces a
large, content-free diff that buries the real changes.

Example diff (from a Buildkite render):

```diff
-# Variable overrides used: {"deploySha":"c1b207207834863ba96ffd130f1b1d03c0406672"}
+# Variable overrides used: {"deploySha":"9d3377a17f4b2806aa0c3ed337ebcb9a70811416"}
```

## Goal

**Remove the `# Variable overrides used: {...}` comment entirely.** It is the source
of the diff noise and provides no durable value (the override values churn every
build). The `# This file is autogenerated from ...` comment is kept, unchanged.

## Out of scope

The same example diff also shows the `# This file is autogenerated from ...` URL
churning, because the linter pipeline renders out of an ephemeral Buildkite clone
(`/buildkite/builds/kite-<uuid>/.../temp_clone_repo/deployment/templates/...`) and
that absolute prefix is embedded verbatim. **This is being fixed on the caller
side** (the linter pipeline will render from a stable, repo-relative path), not in
this gem.

`--variable-override` itself is **not** being removed: it still merges the override
values into the rendered variables (`Template#initialize` does
`variables.merge(variable_overrides)`). Only the comment that echoes those values is
removed.

## Design

The change is confined to `lib/kubernetes_template_rendering/template.rb`:

- In `with_auto_generated_yaml_comment` (template.rb:26-33), drop the
  `variable_override_comment +` term so the output is just the warning header plus
  the YAML body:

  ```ruby
  def with_auto_generated_yaml_comment(yaml_string)
    comment = <<~EOS
    # WARNING: DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE!
    # Any changes made here will be lost.
    # This file is autogenerated from #{full_template_path}
    EOS
    comment + yaml_string
  end
  ```

- Delete the now-unused `variable_override_comment` method (template.rb:43-49).

No call-chain threading is needed. The `variable_overrides` argument continues to
flow through `CLI → TemplateDirectoryRenderer → ResourceSet → Resource → Template`
purely so it can be merged into the rendered variables; that path is untouched. The
`variable_overrides` `attr_reader` can stay (still read by `initialize`) — no need to
remove it.

## Testing

In `spec/kubernetes_template_rendering/template_spec.rb`:

- Remove the assertion at line 56 that expects
  `# Variable overrides used: {"deploySha":"override"}` to be present.
- Add an assertion that the rendered output does **not** include
  `# Variable overrides used:`, even when `variable_overrides` are passed.
- Confirm the `# This file is autogenerated from ...` line is still present.

## Release

This is a behavior change to a released gem (currently `0.2.5`):

- Bump the version to `0.3.0` (`lib/kubernetes_template_rendering/version.rb`).
- Add a `CHANGELOG.md` entry under `## [0.3.0]` with a `### Removed` section noting
  the `# Variable overrides used:` comment is no longer emitted (it caused large,
  content-free diffs because `deploySha` changes every build).
